title.en: You killed it! You're on fire today! 와, 진짜 찢었다! 오늘 완전 날아다니는데?
title.ko: 와, 진짜 찢었다! 오늘 완전 날아다니는데?

description.en: When someone dominates the stage or achieves perfect results! Practice giving trendy compliments with 진짜 찢었다 and 완전 날아다니는구나.
description.ko: 누군가 무대를 장악하거나 완벽한 성과를 냈을 때! 진짜 찢었다와 완전 날아다니는구나를 사용해 트렌디한 칭찬을 건네 봅니다.

tags: Reaction. Compliment. Slang. Success. Positive. Daily Life

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world! Welcome back. You are listening to... "Real Reaction!" I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존! 오늘 오프닝 멘트 정말 대단했어요!
content.translation: John! Today's opening remarks were amazing!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 발음도 완벽하고 에너지도 넘쳐서 진짜 무대를 찢었는데요?
content.translation: The pronunciation was perfect and the energy was so high that you really tore up the stage!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Really? I was a bit nervous. but I tried my best.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 전혀 긴장한 것처럼 안 보였어요. 오늘 완전 날아다니네요!
content.translation: You didn't look nervous at all. John. you're totally on fire today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Thanks. Mina! Your reaction makes me want to do even better.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 찢었다. This sounds like I literally tore something apart. right.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 원래는 종이나 옷을 찢는다는 뜻이지만. 지금은 무대나 공연을 완벽하게 장악했다는 뜻으로 써요.
content.translation: Yes. Originally it means to tear paper or clothes. but now it means to completely dominate a stage or performance.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 기대 이상으로 너무 잘해서 감탄이 나올 때. "와! 진짜 찢었다!" 라고 말하죠.
content.translation: When someone does much better than expected and you're impressed. you say "Wow. you killed it!"

content.speaker: John
content.text: 진짜 찢었다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Then what about 날아다니다.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이건 사람이 새처럼 하늘을 날아다닐 만큼 컨디션이 좋고 실력을 마음껏 발휘한다는 뜻이에요.
content.translation: This means that a person is in such good condition and shows their skills to their heart's content that they seem to be flying like a bird.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 일이 술술 풀리거나 활약이 대단할 때 쓰는 아주 기분 좋은 칭찬입니다.
content.translation: It's a very pleasant compliment used when things are going smoothly or someone's activity is great.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 완전 날아다닌다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. let's try a situation. Imagine Mina just finished a very difficult Korean traditional dance performance.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 저 방금 공연 끝내고 왔어요. 실수 안 하고 잘 마쳤는지 모르겠네요.
content.translation: John. I just finished my performance. I don't know if I finished it well without making mistakes.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina! What are you talking about? You were the star of the show.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 미나 씨. 오늘 진짜 찢었어요! 관객들이 다 일어나서 박수 치는 거 못 봤어요?
content.translation: Mina. you really killed it today! Didn't you see all the audience standing up and clapping?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정말요? 사람들이 좋아해 줬다니 다행이에요.
content.translation: Really? I'm glad people liked it.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 춤추는 모습이 너무 가벼워서 완전 날아다니는 줄 알았어요.
content.translation: You looked so light while dancing that I thought you were totally flying.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 고마워요 그동안 힘들게 연습한 보람이 있네요.
content.translation: Thank you. John! Thanks to you. the hard practice all this time was worth it.

content.speaker: John
content.text: A trendy compliment can give someone a huge confidence boost.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. "잘했다" 라는 말도 좋지만. 오늘 배운 표현들은 훨씬 더 생동감이 느껴지죠.
content.translation: Right. Saying "You did well" is good. but the expressions we learned today feel much more vivid.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 주변에 멋진 활약을 한 친구가 있다면 아낌없이 이 표현들을 써보세요!
content.translation: If there's a friend who did a great job around you. use these expressions generously!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "trendy" compliments one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 진짜 찢었다! You killed it!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 오늘 완전 날아다니네! You're on fire today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 완전 실력 발휘했어! You showed your full potential!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 무대를 장악했어! You dominated the stage!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 오늘 네가 주인공이야! You're the star today!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 하시는 모든 일에서 무대를 찢는 멋진 하루 보내시길 바랄게요.
content.translation: I hope you all have a wonderful day tearing up the stage in everything you do today.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 선생님께 칭찬을 들어서 기분이 아주 좋아요. (I feel very good because I heard a compliment from the teacher.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be amazing. To be great
vocabulary.example: 존의 한국어 실력은 정말 대단해요. (John's Korean skills are truly amazing.)
무대 위에 올라가면 항상 긴장이 돼요. (I always get nervous when I go up on stage.)
그 선수는 경기에 나갈 때마다 실력을 발휘해요. (That player shows his skills every time he goes to a match.)
이 표현은 요즘 친구들 사이에서 큰 유행이에요. (This expression is a big trend among friends these days.)
공연장 분위기가 아주 뜨거웠어요. (The atmosphere of the concert hall was very hot.)
